threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'grocery store' instead of the 'corner shop'—dead giveaway that you're fresh off the boat.
Last night, the 196 bus had a guy singing opera at the back. It was oddly beautiful, but the driver looked like he'd rather be anywhere else.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of old men playing boules in Tempelhofer Feld, laughing over a bottle of homemade wine—that's when it clicked.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a late-night kebab shop that’s also an art gallery. Late-night snack and local art in one go!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know the best taco truck lines are shorter after 9 PM; newcomers just order from the first spot they see.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading commuters on the 38 Geary today, while a woman next to him tried to nap. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the quiet corners and the random pequenas places that serve the best vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always ask for directions to La Sagrada Família like it’s the only place that matters, oblivious to the hidden gems in Gràcia.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Dalston Junction instead of just Junction. Real locals know it’s all about the 'only in Dalston' vibe, not the station.
Last night on the 133, a lad serenaded the whole bus with his off-key rendition of 'Wonderwall'—definitely a moment I won’t forget.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was really living here when I saw a guy serenading a tram full of commuters with a ukulele on Richardstraße. No one even flinched.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery—all while you’re waiting for your döner to be freshly sliced.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they grumble about BART delays but still somehow have a favorite taco spot that’s worth the wait.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy eating a burrito on the 38 while loudly explaining his conspiracy theories to a captive audience. Classic Muni moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just waltz into any bar at 5 PM for a vermut, but locals know it's all about that pre-lunch pint and tapas at noon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newbies; they’re the ones who wait for the pedestrian light even when there are no cars in sight.
